{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a84f376eafc243818f541bc3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: repo-hygiene\n3\tdescription: >-\n4\t  Audit a repository for hygiene debt — committed build artifacts, dead GitHub Actions\n5\t  workflows, README version drift, dirty working trees, stash pileups, missing .gitignore\n6\t  coverage, suspicious tracked files — and optionally fix it with one commit per category.\n7\t  Use to check, audit, or clean up repo hygiene.\n8\targument-hint: \"[repo-path] [--fix]\"\n9\tallowed-tools: Bash, Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion\n10\t---\n11\t\n12\t# Repo Hygiene Skill\n13\t\n14\tDetect (and optionally fix) systemic repository hygiene debt. The detection core is a\n15\tplain, dependency-free bash script — the skill is a thin wrapper around it. The script\n16\tis **report-only**: it never modifies anything. All mutation lives in this skill's fix\n17\tmode and only runs when the user explicitly asks for it.\n18\t\n19\t## Step 1: Run the Audit (Always Report First)\n20\t\n21\tRun the core script against the target repo (argument, or the current repo if none given):\n22\t\n23\t```bash\n24\t~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh [repo-path]\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\tExit codes: `0` clean, `1` findings, `2` usage error / not a git repo.\n28\tAdd `--json` for machine-readable output.\n29\t\n30\tRun this first **even when the user asked for `--fix`** — fixes are always driven from a\n31\tfresh report.\n32\t\n33\t## Step 2: Present Findings\n34\t\n35\tSummarize each category with its severity, count, and a few example paths. Categories:\n36\t\n37\t| Category | Severity | Meaning |\n38\t|----------|----------|---------|\n39\t| `tracked-build-artifacts` | high | `target/`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `.DS_Store` under version control |\n40\t| `large-tracked-files` | high | tracked blobs > 5 MB |\n41\t| `dead-ci-workflows` | medium | tracked `.github/workflows/*.yml\\|yaml` (GH Actions are disabled estate-wide — CI is Argo Workflows) |\n42\t| `readme-dead-ci-badges` | medium | GitHub Actions badge URLs in the README (dead by policy) |\n43\t| `readme-version-drift` | low | version-looking badge strings that don't match the latest git tag |\n44\t| `dirty-working-tree` / `stash-pileup` | low | uncommitted changes / accumulated stashes |\n45\t| `gitignore-gaps` | medium | language detected (Cargo.toml, package.json, Python sources) but its build dir is not ignored |\n46\t| `suspicious-tracked-files` | needs-review | `.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa*` — **never read or print their contents**; flag for the human to decide |\n47\t\n48\t## Step 3: Fix Mode (Only on Explicit `--fix`)\n49\t\n50\tIf — and only if — the user passed `--fix` or explicitly asked for fixes, apply them.\n51\t\n52\t**Preconditions — abort fix mode entirely if any fails:**\n53\t\n54\t- No merge/rebase/cherry-pick in progress: none of `.git/MERGE_HEAD`,\n55\t  `.git/rebase-merge`, `.git/rebase-apply`, `.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD` may exist.\n56\t- The repo must be the one the user pointed at — never fix a repo the user only asked\n57\t  to audit.\n58\t\n59\t**Apply fixes as SEPARATE commits, one per category, strictly limited to:**\n60\t\n61\t1. **`.gitignore` entries** — append the missing entries reported by `gitignore-gaps`.\n62\t2. **Build artifacts** — `git rm -r --cached <paths>` only. **Never delete from disk.**\n63\t   Requires the matching `.gitignore` entry to land first (commit 1) so the files don't\n64\t   immediately reappear as untracked noise.\n65\t3. **Dead CI workflows** — `git rm` the tracked `.github/workflows/*.yml|yaml` files\n66\t   (these are policy-dead; deleting the file from disk is the fix).\n67\t4. **README drift** — correct version badge strings to match the latest tag and remove\n68\t   GitHub Actions badges.\n69\t\n70\tStage with explicit paths only (never `git add -A`), so unrelated dirty-tree changes are\n71\tnever swept into a hygiene commit.\n72\t\n73\t**Never:** touch source code, force-push, rewrite history, drop stashes, delete\n74\tnon-workflow files from disk, or \"fix\" suspicious tracked files — those are report-only.\n75\t`dirty-working-tree` and `stash-pileup` have no automated fix; just report them.\n76\t\n77\tDo not push unless the user asks. Re-run the script after fixing to confirm the\n78\tcategories cleared.\n79\t\n80\t## Fleet Mode (Cross-Harness)\n81\t\n82\t**The script is the cross-harness interface.** NEEDLE-dispatched agents on any harness\n83\t(claude-code, opencode, codex, aider) should invoke it directly and parse the JSON —\n84\tno Claude Code machinery required:\n85\t\n86\t```bash\n87\t~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh --json /path/to/repo\n88\t```\n89\t\n90\tOutput shape:\n91\t\n92\t```json\n93\t{\"repo\": \"...\", \"findings\": [{\"category\": \"...\", \"severity\": \"...\", \"count\": 0, \"examples\": []}], \"clean\": true}\n94\t```\n95\t\n96\tThe script needs only `git`, coreutils, and `awk` (no jq), so it runs identically under\n97\tevery harness. The SKILL.md you are reading only adds presentation and the guarded fix\n98\tworkflow on top.\n99\t\n100\t## Optional: SessionEnd Hook (Default OFF)\n101\t\n102\tFor an automatic hygiene report when a Claude Code session ends, a user can opt in by\n103\tadding this to their `settings.json`. It is **not installed by default** — report-only,\n104\tand `|| true` keeps a findings exit code from being treated as a hook failure:\n105\t\n106\t```json\n107\t{\n108\t  \"hooks\": {\n109\t    \"SessionEnd\": [\n110\t      {\n111\t        \"hooks\": [\n112\t          {\n113\t            \"type\": \"command\",\n114\t            \"command\": \"~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh . || true\"\n115\t          }\n116\t        ]\n117\t      }\n118\t    ]\n119\t  }\n120\t}\n121\t```\n122\t\n123\tDo not add this hook yourself; only mention it if the user asks about automation.\n124\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f2903c5-47be-413e-80eb-67322b2777cb","uuid":"595711fc-19d0-4439-a135-3784f03472d4","timestamp":"2026-07-20T00:33:18.037Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/jeds-curated-skills/repo-hygiene/SKILL.md","content":"---\nname: repo-hygiene\ndescription: >-\n  Audit a repository for hygiene debt — committed build artifacts, dead GitHub Actions\n  workflows, README version drift, dirty working trees, stash pileups, missing .gitignore\n  coverage, suspicious tracked files — and optionally fix it with one commit per category.\n  Use to check, audit, or clean up repo hygiene.\nargument-hint: \"[repo-path] [--fix]\"\nallowed-tools: Bash, Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion\n---\n\n# Repo Hygiene Skill\n\nDetect (and optionally fix) systemic repository hygiene debt. The detection core is a\nplain, dependency-free bash script — the skill is a thin wrapper around it. The script\nis **report-only**: it never modifies anything. All mutation lives in this skill's fix\nmode and only runs when the user explicitly asks for it.\n\n## Step 1: Run the Audit (Always Report First)\n\nRun the core script against the target repo (argument, or the current repo if none given):\n\n```bash\n~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh [repo-path]\n```\n\nExit codes: `0` clean, `1` findings, `2` usage error / not a git repo.\nAdd `--json` for machine-readable output.\n\nRun this first **even when the user asked for `--fix`** — fixes are always driven from a\nfresh report.\n\n## Step 2: Present Findings\n\nSummarize each category with its severity, count, and a few example paths. Categories:\n\n| Category | Severity | Meaning |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `tracked-build-artifacts` | high | `target/`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `.DS_Store` under version control |\n| `large-tracked-files` | high | tracked blobs > 5 MB |\n| `dead-ci-workflows` | medium | tracked `.github/workflows/*.yml\\|yaml` (GH Actions are disabled estate-wide — CI is Argo Workflows) |\n| `readme-dead-ci-badges` | medium | GitHub Actions badge URLs in the README (dead by policy) |\n| `readme-version-drift` | low | version-looking badge strings that don't match the latest git tag |\n| `dirty-working-tree` / `stash-pileup` | low | uncommitted changes / accumulated stashes |\n| `gitignore-gaps` | medium | language detected (Cargo.toml, package.json, Python sources) but its build dir is not ignored |\n| `suspicious-tracked-files` | needs-review | `.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa*` — **never read or print their contents**; flag for the human to decide |\n\n## Step 3: Fix Mode (Only on Explicit `--fix`)\n\nIf — and only if — the user passed `--fix` or explicitly asked for fixes, apply them.\n\n**Preconditions — abort fix mode entirely if any fails:**\n\n- No merge/rebase/cherry-pick in progress: none of `.git/MERGE_HEAD`,\n  `.git/rebase-merge`, `.git/rebase-apply`, `.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD` may exist.\n- The repo must be the one the user pointed at — never fix a repo the user only asked\n  to audit.\n\n**Apply fixes as SEPARATE commits, one per category, strictly limited to:**\n\n1. **`.gitignore` entries** — append the missing entries reported by `gitignore-gaps`.\n2. **Build artifacts** — `git rm -r --cached <paths>` only. **Never delete from disk.**\n   Requires the matching `.gitignore` entry to land first (commit 1) so the files don't\n   immediately reappear as untracked noise.\n3. **Dead CI workflows** — `git rm` the tracked `.github/workflows/*.yml|yaml` files\n   (these are policy-dead; deleting the file from disk is the fix).\n4. **README drift** — correct version badge strings to match the latest tag and remove\n   GitHub Actions badges.\n\nStage with explicit paths only (never `git add -A`), so unrelated dirty-tree changes are\nnever swept into a hygiene commit.\n\n**Never:** touch source code, force-push, rewrite history, drop stashes, delete\nnon-workflow files from disk, or \"fix\" suspicious tracked files — those are report-only.\n`dirty-working-tree` and `stash-pileup` have no automated fix; just report them.\n\nDo not push unless the user asks. Re-run the script after fixing to confirm the\ncategories cleared.\n\n## Fleet Mode (Cross-Harness)\n\n**The script is the cross-harness interface.** NEEDLE-dispatched agents on any harness\n(claude-code, opencode, codex, aider) should invoke it directly and parse the JSON —\nno Claude Code machinery required:\n\n```bash\n~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh --json /path/to/repo\n```\n\nOutput shape:\n\n```json\n{\"repo\": \"...\", \"findings\": [{\"category\": \"...\", \"severity\": \"...\", \"count\": 0, \"examples\": []}], \"clean\": true}\n```\n\nThe script needs only `git`, coreutils, and `awk` (no jq), so it runs identically under\nevery harness. The SKILL.md you are reading only adds presentation and the guarded fix\nworkflow on top.\n\n## Optional: SessionEnd Hook (Default OFF)\n\nFor an automatic hygiene report when a Claude Code session ends, a user can opt in by\nadding this to their `settings.json`. It is **not installed by default** — report-only,\nand `|| true` keeps a findings exit code from being treated as a hook failure:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"SessionEnd\": [\n      {\n        \"hooks\": [\n          {\n            \"type\": \"command\",\n            \"command\": \"~/.claude/skills/repo-hygiene/scripts/repo_hygiene.sh . || true\"\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\nDo not add this hook yourself; only mention it if the user asks about automation.\n","numLines":124,"startLine":1,"totalLines":124}}}
